samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Parallel to serial converter, converts every 4 parallel inputs to 1 serial output (MSB first).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial output representing the converted data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="IDLE">0</constant>
        <constant id="CONVERT">1</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[1:0]">Counter register to keep track of the current bit being processed.</reg>
        <reg id="data" width_description="[3:0]">Register to hold the current parallel data.</reg>
        <reg id="valid">Valid signal to indicate the availability of serial output.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Assign the most significant bit of the parallel input to the serial output
        assign dout = data[3];
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the active low reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            // Check if the reset signal is active
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset the counter register to 0
                cnt <= 0;
                
                // Set the valid signal to 0
                valid <= 0;
            end else begin
                // Check if the counter register has reached its maximum value (3)
                if (cnt == 3) begin
                    // Update the data register with the parallel input
                    data <= d;
                    
                    // Reset the counter register to 0
                    cnt <= 0;
                    
                    // Set the valid signal to 1
                    valid <= 1;
                end else begin
                    // Increment the counter register by 1
                    cnt <= cnt + 1;
                    
                    // Set the valid signal to 0
                    valid <= 0;
                    
                    // Shift the data register to the left by 1 position
                    data <= {data[2:0], data[3]};
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign the valid signal to the valid_out port
        assign valid_out = valid;
    </implementation>
    
    <!-- Task to generate testbench code -->
    <task id="generate_testbench">
        <description>Generate testbench code for the parallel2serial module.</description>
        <step>Write test cases for all possible combinations of parallel inputs.</step>
        <step>Check the validity of the serial output for each combination.</step>
        <step>Verify that the valid_out signal is set correctly for each case.</step>
    </task>
endmodule
